title: Docassemble
description: Open-source expert system for guided interviews and document assembly, widely used to build legal-aid and law-firm self-help applications.
installation: Docassemble ships as a single all-in-one image (jhpyle/docassemble) whose roles are selected with the CONTAINERROLE env var. This template runs the web role and externalises the SQL database and Redis to the bundled add-ons. ROTATE the Postgres password and SECRETKEY (openssl rand -base64 32) before production. The container listens on internal port 80; set BEHINDHTTPSLOADBALANCER=true because Kubero's ingress terminates TLS, and DAHOSTNAME is auto-set from {{KUBERO_APP_HOST}}. On first boot the schema is created automatically; the bootstrap admin is admin@admin.com / password (set/rotate via the configuration). Persistent application data (uploaded packages, generated files, certs, config.yml) lives under /usr/share/docassemble on the volume. COMPANION SERVICES: for a production multi-server install deploy additional replicas of the SAME jhpyle/docassemble image with different CONTAINERROLE values that share the same Postgres, Redis and /usr/share/docassemble volume: CONTAINERROLE=celery (background task / document-assembly worker), CONTAINERROLE=cron (scheduled tasks), and optionally CONTAINERROLE=log (centralised logging) and CONTAINERROLE=mail. RabbitMQ is only needed for the SQL/Redis-replicated HA topology (set RABBITMQ=pyamqp://...) and can be added later; a single-node web+celery setup does not require it. A background OCR/translation ML container is not required for basic operation. To externalise file storage to S3/Azure instead of the volume, set the S3 or AZURE config keys in the docassemble configuration (not an operator add-on).
